Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

commit.message:
Fused matmul+ReLU kernel and B=48: 23.93 TFLOPS (95.7% peak)

Two optimizations that nearly saturate the hardware:

1. Fused C = ReLU(A × B) kernel (matmul_relu.cc):
   - Replaces 3 separate calls (zero + matmul + relu) with 1 fused call
   - Zero-initializes accumulators in registers (no memory round-trip)
   - Applies ReLU during store phase (no separate load/store pass)
   - Reduces hardware loop body from 6 to 2 kernel dispatches

2. Increased batch size B=16 → B=48:
   - Matmul outer loop goes from 1 to 3 iterations (M/(2r) = 48/16 = 3)
   - Much better SIMD pipeline utilization
   - SRAM: 32KB (W) + 12KB×2 (act) + 1KB (stack) = 57KB < 64KB

Combined result: 8.98 → 23.93 TFLOPS (2.67× improvement)
Per-tile: 374 → 997 GFLOPS (2.67× improvement)
CPU speedup: 18.5× → 25.9×

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: /process and /bind now create real policy + billing records

/process endpoint (full AI workflow):
- Triage → updates submission status to 'triaged'
- Underwriting → extracts premium from AI response, sets 'quoted'
- Auto-bind if premium <\ → creates policy + billing records
- Above \ → leaves as 'quoted_pending_approval' for human
- Compliance → logs DecisionRecord with full workflow audit
- Domain events published at each step

/bind endpoint:
- Creates real policy record in repository (was generating fake UUID)
- Creates billing account with balance_due = premium
- Publishes policy.bound domain event

Both endpoints now produce actual data that appears in
Policies list, Billing, Events, and Compliance views.

* fix(mcp): await bridge init so tools are registered before plugin returns

The fire-and-forget IIFE populated mcpToolEntries via Object.assign after
the plugin had already returned a spread-copy of the (empty) object.
MCP tools were never actually available to the model.

Fix: await the MCP init directly in the plugin's async function body so
tools are discovered and registered before the tool hook is returned.

Also replaces the fragile toString()-based lazy-load check in
McpClientManager with a simple null check.

* feat(mcp): inject tool definitions into chat.params and system message

The tool hook only registers execution handlers — it does not add tool
definitions to the model's tool list. This commit:

1. Adds buildMcpToolDefinitions() to generate function-call format defs
2. Injects them into output.options.tools in chat.params
3. Updates experimental.chat.system.transform to include MCP tool names

Verified: all 7 MCP tools now reach the proxy in the 33-tool request.
The model sees them in tool definitions but the runtime's authoritative
system prompt does not list them, so the model currently will not call
them. This is a runtime limitation, not a plugin bug.

* feat(mcp): add mcptool CLI for Shell-based MCP tool execution

Root cause: the agent binary has 15 fixed internal tools and cannot
call plugin-registered tools directly. Solution: mcptool CLI that the
model invokes via Shell to call any configured MCP server tool.

- Add src/cli/mcptool.ts (servers, tools, call subcommands)
- Update buildAvailableToolsSystemMessage with Shell usage instructions
- Add mcptool binary to package.json bin + build entries
- Update tests for new system message format

commit.message:
Fix podcast notification reappearing after Stop is tapped (#101)

* Initial plan

* Fix podcast playback notification reappearing after stop button is tapped

Two bugs caused the podcast notification to reappear after the user
tapped Stop:

1. Race condition in loadStationLogoAndUpdateNotification(): the
   background Thread that loads artwork via Glide could call
   notificationManager.notify() AFTER stopPlayback() had already
   called stopForeground() + nm.cancel(). Because the notify() was
   issued directly from the background thread, it raced with the
   main-thread cancel. Fix: post the notify() call to the main-thread
   handler and re-check isStopped inside the post so it is serialised
   with stopPlayback().

2. podcastProgressRunnable's finally block called
   handler.postDelayed(this, 500) unconditionally. In Kotlin (like
   Java) a finally block runs even when an early `return` is executed,
   so after stopPlayback() set isStopped = true the runnable kept
   re-queuing itself every 500ms indefinitely. Fix: guard the
   postDelayed call with if (!isStopped).

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sunburst center click, deeper tree fetch, and LRU cache

Three fixes:

1. Center click broken: SVG <text> elements overlaid the <circle> and
   swallowed click events. Added pointer-events:none to both text
   elements so clicks pass through to the circle. Also added a small
   '▲ UP' indicator when drilled down.

2. Shallow data: increased FETCH_DEPTH from 5 to 8 so nested folders
   are covered. Now passes showHidden prop through from workspace-
   explorer so hidden files are included when the toggle is on.

3. No caching: every folder selection re-fetched the deep tree. Added a
   16-entry LRU cache keyed by path+showHidden, invalidated on
   workspace-update events.

fix(schema-org): allow null to opt out of default values (#680)

* fix(schema-org): allow null to opt out of default values

When a user sets a schema-org property to null, it is now removed from
the final output instead of being overwritten by setIfEmpty defaults or
inheritMeta. null acts as an explicit opt-out sentinel that is preserved
through the full resolution pipeline and stripped only after all
resolvers (including resolveRootNode) have run.

Closes #548

commit.message:
Feature/lev missing runtime sim (#92)

* bd: backup 2026-03-07 09:53

* feat: dynamic context reconstruction research — 5 gap issues created

Deep research into how Leviathan's harness can computationally reconstruct
context as a living 3D graph matrix (past gradients, present entities,
projected futures) instead of append-only logs.

- Cursor IDE context techniques analysis
- Comparative analysis: MemGPT, GraphRAG, Generative Agents, LangGraph, Mastra, RAPTOR, Zep
- 5 gaps identified: ClassifyFn V1, SessionAggregate, Context Projection Engine,
  Temporal Projection, Progressive Disclosure
- BD issues: lev-nwa → lev-pel → lev-z9e → lev-v02/lev-uxq
- Design doc in .context/dynamic-context-design.md (gitignored, local only)

* feat(graph): ClassifyFn V1 + Context Projection Engine

ClassifyFn V1 (lev-nwa):
- Entity temperature scoring: f(recency, depth, claims, links, session)
- Exponential decay with configurable half-life (mirrors FlowMind DecayConfig)
- Temperature tiers: hot/warm/cool/cold with depth auto-promotion
- Backwards-compatible: preserves LWW merge, adds scoring layer on top
- Active reference tracking for conversation-aware scoring

Context Projection Engine (lev-z9e):
- Unified context assembly from entity graph with past/present/future tiers
- Token budgeting with configurable tier splits and output reserve
- Progressive disclosure: hot entities rendered deeper, cold entities shallower
- Graph-proximity boost: neighbors of hot entities get 30% score boost
- Zoom levels L0-L3 map to entity render depth

Tests: 15/15 passing (10 temperature, 5 projector)

* bd: backup 2026-03-07 16:29

* bd: backup 2026-03-07 16:30

* bd: backup 2026-03-07 20:30

* feat(sim): add simulation harness exercising all runtime use cases

Interactive simulation binary (lev-sim) that exercises:
- UC1: Declaration store with scope hierarchy, domains, capabilities,
  connectors, bindings, archetypes, views, validation, versioning
- UC2: Policy engine with 4 test cases (staging allow, prod deny,
  deny-by-default, capability-attached allow)
- UC3: Full execution pipeline through mock Claude Agent SDK and
  Motia adapters, including deny-by-default blocking
- UC4: Provenance log inspection, time-travel replay with execution

feat: Multi-session view - show ALL active agents and sessions (#264) (#6)

Implements GitHub Issue #264 by adding multi-session monitoring:

- Session Overview Panel: Shows all active Agency and Copilot CLI sessions
- Displays active session count, total PIDs, and MCP server count
- Session table with age, last write time, and session type
- Merged Activity Feed: Chronologically combines tool calls from all sessions
- Color-coded sessions for easy visual distinction
- Scans ~/.agency/logs and ~/.copilot/logs for sessions active in last 30min
- Extracts and tags entries with session identifier

This provides comprehensive visibility into all concurrent agent activities
rather than just showing the single most recent log.
